L. 107–107, § 921(b)(2)“(b) .—(1) Not less than once every three years, or upon the request of the President or the Secretary of Defense, the Chairman shall submit to the Secretary of Defense a report containing such recommendations for changes in the assignment of functions (or roles and missions) to the armed forces as the Chairman considers necessary to achieve maximum effectiveness of the armed forces. In preparing each such report, the Chairman shall consider (among other matters) the following:Report on Assignment of Roles and Missions“(A) Changes in the nature of the threats faced by the United States.“(B) Unnecessary duplication of effort among the armed forces.“(C) Changes in technology that can be applied effectively to warfare.“(2) The Chairman shall include in each such report recommendations for such changes in policies, directives, regulations, and legislation as may be necessary to achieve the changes in the assignment of functions recommended by the Chairman.”2000—Subsec. (d)(1). , title IX, § 905(b)], substituted “At or about the time that the budget is submitted to Congress for a fiscal year under ,” for “Not later than August 15 of each year,” in introductory provisions.Pub. Termination of Reporting RequirementsFor termination, effective , of provisions in subsec. (c) of this section requiring submittal of annual report to Congress, see , set out as a note under .Dec. 31, 2021section 1061 of Pub. L. 114–328section 111 of this title

Inclusion of Assessment of Joint Military Training and Force Allocations in Quadrennial Defense Review and National Military Strategy, , , provided that:Pub. L. 112–81, div. A, title III, § 348125 Stat. 1375Dec. 31, 2011“The assessments of the National Military Strategy conducted by the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff under [sic; probably means Title 10, Armed Forces], and the quadrennial roles and missions review pursuant to [former] [sic], shall include an assessment of joint military training and force allocations to determine—section 153(b) of this titlesection 118b of this title“(1)the compliance of the military departments with the joint training, doctrine, and resource allocation recommendations promulgated by the Joint Chiefs of Staff; and“(2)the effectiveness of the Joint Staff in carrying out the missions of planning and experimentation formerly accomplished by Joint Forces Command.”

Common Measurement of Operations Tempo and Personnel Tempo, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–85, div. A, title III, § 326111 Stat. 1679Nov. 18, 1997“(a).—Means for MeasurementThe Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff shall, to the maximum extent practicable, develop (1) a common means of measuring the operations tempo (OPTEMPO) of each of the Armed Forces, and (2) a common means of measuring the personnel tempo (PERSTEMPO) of each of the Armed Forces. The Chairman shall consult with the other members of the Joint Chiefs of Staff in developing those common means of measurement.“(b).—PERSTEMPO MeasurementThe measurement of personnel tempo developed by the Chairman shall include a means of identifying the rate of deployment for individual members of the Armed Forces in addition to the rate of deployment for units.”

Annual Assessment of Force Readiness, , , provided for an annual assessment of readiness and capability of the Armed Forces by the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff to be submitted to Congress not later than March 1 of each of 1994, 1995, and 1996 and for interim assessments between annual submissions in the event of a significant change in readiness or capability of the Armed Forces.Pub. L. 103–160, div. A, title III, § 376107 Stat. 1637Nov. 30, 1993

Report of Chairman of Joint Chiefs of Staff on Roles and Missions of Armed Forces, , , provided for the Secretary of Defense to transmit to Congress a copy of the first report relating to the roles and missions of the Armed Forces that was submitted by the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff under subsec. (b) of this section after , and directed the Chairman to include in the report comments and recommendations.Pub. L. 102–484, div. A, title IX, § 901106 Stat. 2469Oct. 23, 1992Jan. 1, 1992

Transition Provisions, (b), , , provided dates for establishment of the uniform system of evaluating the preparedness of each unified and specified combatant command and for submission of the first report.Pub. L. 99–433, title II, § 204(a)100 Stat. 1011Oct. 1, 1986

children.heading: .—Strategic direction
children.label: 1 .—Strategic direction
children.text: Assisting the President and the Secretary in providing for the strategic direction of the armed forces.

children.label: A
children.text: developing strategic frameworks and preparing strategic plans, as required, to guide the use and employment of military force and related activities across all geographic regions and military functions and domains, and to sustain military efforts over different durations of time, as necessary;

children.label: B
children.text: advising the Secretary on the production of the national defense strategy required by and the national security strategy required by section 108 of the National Security Act of 1947 ();section 113(g) of this title50 U.S.C. 3043

children.label: C
children.text: preparing military analysis, options, and plans, as the Chairman considers appropriate, to recommend to the President and the Secretary;

children.label: D
children.text: providing for the preparation and review of contingency plans which conform to policy guidance from the President and the Secretary; and

children.label: E
children.text: preparing joint logistic and mobility plans to support national defense strategies and recommending the assignment of responsibilities to the armed forces in accordance with such plans.

children.label: A
children.text: providing advice to the President and the Secretary on ongoing military operations; and

children.label: B
children.text: advising the Secretary on the allocation and transfer of forces among geographic and functional combatant commands, as necessary, to address transregional, multi-domain, and multifunctional threats.

children.label: A
children.text: evaluating the overall preparedness of the joint force to perform the responsibilities of that force under national defense strategies and to respond to significant contingencies worldwide;

children.label: B
children.text: assessing the risks to United States missions, strategies, and military personnel that stem from shortfalls in military readiness across the armed forces, and developing risk mitigation options;

children.label: C
children.text: advising the Secretary on critical deficiencies and strengths in joint force capabilities (including manpower, logistics, and mobility support) identified during the preparation and review of national defense strategies and contingency plans and assessing the effect of such deficiencies and strengths on meeting national security objectives and policy and on strategic plans;

children.label: D
children.text: advising the Secretary on the missions and functions that are likely to require contractor or other external support to meet national security objectives and policy and strategy, and the risks associated with such support; and

children.label: E
children.text: establishing and maintaining, after consultation with the commanders of the unified and specified combatant commands, a uniform system of evaluating the preparedness of each such command, and groups of commands collectively, to carry out missions assigned to the command or commands.

children.label: A
children.text: identifying new joint military capabilities based on advances in technology and concepts of operation needed to maintain the technological and operational superiority of the armed forces, and recommending investments and experiments in such capabilities to the Secretary;

children.label: B
children.text: performing military net assessments of the joint capabilities of the armed forces of the United States and its allies in comparison with the capabilities of potential adversaries;

children.label: C
children.text: advising the Secretary under on the priorities of the requirements identified by the commanders of the unified and specified combatant commands;section 163(b)(2) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: advising the Secretary on the extent to which the program recommendations and budget proposals of the military departments and other components of the Department of Defense for a fiscal year conform with the priorities established in national defense strategies and with the priorities established for the requirements of the unified and specified combatant commands;

children.label: E
children.text: advising the Secretary on new and alternative joint military capabilities, and alternative program recommendations and budget proposals, within projected resource levels and guidance provided by the Secretary, in order to achieve greater conformance with the priorities referred to in subparagraph (D);

children.label: F
children.text: assessing joint military capabilities and identifying, approving, and prioritizing gaps in such capabilities to meet national defense strategies, pursuant to ; andsection 181 of this title

children.label: G
children.text: recommending to the Secretary appropriate trade-offs among life-cycle cost, schedule, performance, and procurement quantity objectives in the acquisition of materiel and equipment to support the strategic and contingency plans required by this paragraph in the most effective and efficient manner.

children.label: A
children.text: developing doctrine for the joint employment of the armed forces;

children.label: B
children.text: formulating policies and technical standards, and executing actions, for the joint training of the armed forces;

children.label: C
children.text: formulating policies for coordinating the military education of members of the armed forces;

children.label: D
children.text: formulating policies for development and experimentation on both urgent and long-term concepts for joint force employment, including establishment of a process within the Joint Staff for analyzing and prioritizing gaps in capabilities that could potentially be addressed by joint concept development using existing or modified joint force capabilities;

children.label: E
children.text: formulating policies for gathering, developing, and disseminating joint lessons learned for the armed forces; and

children.label: F
children.text: advising the Secretary on development of joint command, control, communications, and cybercapability, including integration and interoperability of such capability, through requirements, integrated architectures, data standards, and assessments.

children.label: A
children.text: recommending to the Secretary, in accordance with , a budget proposal for activities of each unified and specified combatant command;section 166 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: providing for representation of the United States on the Military Staff Committee of the United Nations in accordance with the Charter of the United Nations; and

children.label: C
children.text: performing such other duties as may be prescribed by law or by the President or the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: The Chairman shall determine each even-numbered year whether to prepare a new National Military Strategy in accordance with this paragraph or to update a strategy previously prepared in accordance with this paragraph. The Chairman shall provide such National Military Strategy or update to the Secretary of Defense in time for transmittal to Congress pursuant to paragraph (3), including in time for inclusion in the report of the Secretary of Defense, if any, under paragraph (4).

children.label: B
children.text: Each National Military Strategy (or update) under this paragraph shall be based on a comprehensive review conducted by the Chairman in conjunction with the other members of the Joint Chiefs of Staff and the commanders of the unified and specified combatant commands. Each update shall address only those parts of the most recent National Military Strategy for which the Chairman determines, on the basis of the review, that a modification is needed.

children.label: i
children.text: the most recent National Security Strategy prescribed by the President pursuant to section 108 of the National Security Act of 1947 ();50 U.S.C. 3043

children.label: ii
children.text: the most recent annual report of the Secretary of Defense submitted to the President and Congress pursuant to ;section 113 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: the most recent national defense strategy presented by the Secretary of Defense pursuant to ;section 113 of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: the most recent policy guidance provided by the Secretary of Defense pursuant to ; andsection 113(g) of this title

children.label: v
children.text: any other national security or defense strategic guidance issued by the President or the Secretary of Defense.

children.label: i
children.text: assess the strategic environment, threats, opportunities, and challenges that affect the national security of the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: assess military ends, ways, and means to support the objectives referred to in subparagraph (C);

children.label: iii
children.text: provide the framework for the assessment by the Chairman of military risk, and for the development of risk mitigation options;

children.label: iv
children.text: develop military options to address threats and opportunities;

children.label: v
children.text: assess joint force capabilities, capacities, and resources; and

children.label: vi
children.text: establish military guidance for the development of the joint force and the total force building on guidance by the President and the Secretary of Defense as referred to in subparagraph (C).

children.label: A
children.text: The Chairman shall prepare each year an assessment of the risks associated with the most current National Military Strategy (or update) under paragraph (1). The risk assessment shall be known as the “Risk Assessment of the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff”. The Chairman shall complete preparation of the Risk Assessment in time for transmittal to Congress pursuant to paragraph (3), including in time for inclusion in the report of the Secretary of Defense, if any, under paragraph (4).

children.label: i
children.text: As the Chairman considers appropriate, update any changes to the strategic environment, threats, objectives, force planning and sizing constructs, assessments, and assumptions that informed the National Military Strategy (or update) required by this section.

children.label: ii
children.text: Identify and define the military strategic risks to United States interests and military risks in executing the National Military Strategy (or update).

children.label: iii
children.text: Identify and define levels of risk, including an identification of what constitutes “significant” risk in the judgment of the Chairman.

children.label: I
children.text: Identify and assess risk in the National Military Strategy (or update) by category and level and the ways in which risk might manifest itself, including how risk is projected to increase, decrease, or remain stable over time; and

children.label: II
children.text: for each category of risk, assess the extent to which current or future risk increases, decreases, or is stable as a result of budgetary priorities, tradeoffs, or fiscal constraints or limitations as currently estimated and applied in the current future-years defense program under .section 221 of this title

children.label: v
children.text: Identify and assess risk associated with the assumptions or plans of the National Military Strategy (or update) about the contributions of external support, as appropriate.

children.label: vi
children.text: Identify and assess the critical deficiencies and strengths in force capabilities (including manpower, logistics, intelligence, and mobility support) identified during the preparation and review of the contingency plans of each unified combatant command, and identify and assess the effect of such deficiencies and strengths for the National Military Strategy (or update).

children.label: vii
children.text: Identify and assess risk resulting from, or likely to result from, current or projected effects on military installation resilience.

children.heading: .—Submittal of national military strategy and risk assessment to congress
children.label: 3 .—Submittal of national military strategy and risk assessment to congress
A
children.text: Not later than February 15 of each even-numbered year, the Chairman shall, through the Secretary of Defense, submit to the Committees on Armed Services of the Senate and the House of Representatives the National Military Strategy or update, if any, prepared under paragraph (1) in such year.

children.label: B
children.text: Not later than February 15 each year, the Chairman shall, through the Secretary of Defense, submit to the Committees on Armed Services of the Senate and the House of Representatives the Risk Assessment prepared under paragraph (2) in such year.

children.label: C
children.text: The National Military Strategy (or update) and Risk Assessment submitted under this subsection shall be classified in form, but shall include an unclassified summary.

children.label: A
children.text: In transmitting a National Military Strategy (or update) or Risk Assessment to Congress pursuant to paragraph (3), the Secretary of Defense shall include in the transmittal such comments of the Secretary thereon, if any, as the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: address the risk assumed in the National Military Strategy (or update) concerned, and the additional actions taken or planned to be taken to address such risk using only current technology and force structure capabilities; and

children.label: ii
children.text: specify, for each risk addressed, the extent of, and a schedule for expected mitigation of, such risk, and an assessment of the potential for residual risk, if any, after mitigation.

children.heading: .—Annual Report on Combatant Command Requirements
children.label: c .—Annual Report on Combatant Command Requirements

children.text: Not later than 25 days after the date on which the budget of the President for a fiscal year is submitted to Congress pursuant to , the Chairman shall submit to the congressional defense committees a report on the requirements of the combatant commands established under .section 1105(a) of title 31section 161 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: A consolidation of the integrated priority lists of requirements of the combatant commands.

children.label: B
children.text: The Chairman’s views on the consolidated lists.

children.label: C
children.text: A description of the extent to which the most recent future-years defense program (under ) addresses the requirements on the consolidated lists.section 221 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: A description of the funding proposed in the President’s budget for the next fiscal year, and for the subsequent fiscal years covered by the most recent future-years defense program, to address each deficiency in readiness identified during the joint readiness review conducted under for the first quarter of the current fiscal year.section 117 of this title

Not later than 15 days after the submission of the risk assessment required under subsection (b)(2) or March 1 of each even-numbered year, whichever is earlier, the Chairman shall provide to the Committees on Armed Services of the Senate and the House of Representatives a briefing on the activities of the Chairman under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: a detailed review of the risk assessment required under paragraph (2) of subsection (b), including how such risk assessment addresses the elements required in subparagraph (B) of such paragraph;

children.label: B
children.text: an analysis of how the risk assessment informs and supports other Joint Staff assessments, including joint capability development assessments, joint force development assessments, comprehensive joint readiness assessments, and global military integration assessments; and

children.label: C
children.text: if the risk assessment is not delivered at or before the time of the briefing, a timeline for when the risk assessment will be submitted to the Committees on Armed Services of the Senate and the House of Representatives.
